title: Hair loss

red_flags.text: Supports clinician-documented psychological support, formal intervention or referral, and suicide-risk assessment when indicated.
red_flags.rationale: Supports mapped psychological support and safety assessment only when indicated and clinician-documented.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Supports clinician-documented psychological support, formal intervention or referral, and suicide-risk assessment when indicated.

source.title: British Association of Dermatologists living guideline for managing people with alopecia areata 2024

exact_section.heading: Recommendations R46–R49 — psychological distress and safety

investigations.text: Describes patchy or diffuse hair loss, scalp and body distribution, exclamation-mark hairs, nail involvement, trichoscopy, scarring and scaling differentials, hair-pull context, and case-by-case testing when diagnosis or comorbidity warrants it.
investigations.rationale: Supports mapped patchy or diffuse loss, scalp distribution, scale or scarring differential, hair-pull, trichoscopy, nail findings, and case-by-case thyroid or prior-record review.
investigations.evidence_extract: Describes patchy or diffuse hair loss, scalp and body distribution, exclamation-mark hairs, nail involvement, trichoscopy, scarring and scaling differentials, hair-pull context, and case-by-case testing when diagnosis or comorbidity warrants it.

source.title: British Association of Dermatologists living guideline for managing people with alopecia areata 2024

exact_section.heading: Sections 5.0–6.2 — presentation, differential diagnosis, and investigations

medication.text: Supports clinician-led assessment, shared priorities, severity tracking, expectation discussion, treatment selection, monitoring, and patient information without authorising autonomous treatment output.
medication.rationale: Supports mapped clinician-led management, shared priorities, medication decisions, expectation discussion, information, and arranged follow-up without doses.
medication.evidence_extract: Supports clinician-led assessment, shared priorities, severity tracking, expectation discussion, treatment selection, monitoring, and patient information without authorising autonomous treatment output.

source.title: British Association of Dermatologists living guideline for managing people with alopecia areata 2024

referral.text: Supports full history including site, type, extent, stability, onset, progression, triggers, quality of life, psychological impact, family autoimmune history, photography, severity assessment, and specialist referral criteria.
referral.rationale: Supports mapped alopecia-areata history, onset, progression, distribution, triggers, quality-of-life, family autoimmune history, severity, records, referral, and monitoring fields.
referral.evidence_extract: Supports full history including site, type, extent, stability, onset, progression, triggers, quality of life, psychological impact, family autoimmune history, photography, severity assessment, and specialist referral criteria.

source_limitations: Androgenetic alopecia, broad balding or receding-hairline terms, scalp pain or itch, hormonal history, systemic symptoms, iron and vitamin testing, scalp infection, hair-care advice, and fixed three-month review timing remain unsupported.
Alopecia type, severity, and treatment remain clinician-stated.
Qualified clinician review has not been completed.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
